best_point.open_meteo_history_validation_text: Open-Meteo archive (2023-04-05 → 2026-04-04): mean_temp_c: run 13.4 °C vs archive 16.0 °C (Δ 2.6) rainfall_mm: run 172 mm/yr eq. vs archive 746 mm/yr eq. (rel Δ 77%) humidity_pct: run 66% vs archive 71% (Δ 6) frost_risk: run 0.000 vs archive 0.000 (Δ 0.000)
best_point.rules_reasoning:
Best-ranked site P003 for Tobacco scores 74.3% (good), based on matching weather and soil signals to crop-specific ideal and limit ranges. Confidence reflects how many inputs were present (about 89% here).

What looks strong: Relative humidity (65.6%) is within the ideal band (46.87–70.87) for Tobacco. Clay content (28.1%) is within the ideal band (16–40) for Tobacco. Frost exposure (0.0% of hours at or below freezing) is within the ideal band (0–0.5) for Tobacco. Rainfall (172 mm (annualized)) is within the ideal band (150–1400) for Tobacco. Soil pH (7.53) is within the ideal band (5–8.5) for Tobacco. Soil organic carbon (10.2 g/kg) is within the ideal band (4–50) for Tobacco. Wind speed (13.6 km/h) is within the ideal band (0–40) for Tobacco. (+1 more positives)

What is acceptable but not ideal: Mean temperature is 13.4 °C, below the ideal range (24.94–34.94) but still inside acceptable bounds (11.94–47.94), so growth potential may be reduced.

Data limitations: no values for Surface soil moisture, Sand content, Reference evapotranspiration (ET0). Those gaps reduce confidence and apply a scoring penalty.

top_candidates.llm_reasoning: The strongest positive factor is the zero frost risk, ensuring a long growing season. However, the strongest limiting factor is critically low rainfall (307.33mm), necessitating extensive and costly irrigation. Mean temperature (14.89°C) is suboptimal, potentially slowing growth. Soil pH (7.41) is alkaline, and clay content (28.8%) is higher than ideal, impacting nutrient availability and drainage. Low organic carbon further limits soil health. Operational risks include high irrigation costs, nutrient management challenges, and potential for reduced yield/quality. Mitigation requires intensive water management, soil amendments, and careful variety selection.

score_explanation: Score combines weather and soil fit against crop-specific ideal ranges, then applies calibrated penalties for missing features and hard-threshold violations. Score bands: poor (<40), fair (40-59), good (60-79), excellent (80-100).
